text: After this, David defeated the Philistines and subdued them, taking Metheg-ammah out of the hand of the Philistines.

He also defeated Moab, and measured the captives off with a cord, making them lie down on the ground: two lengths were put to death, and one full length was spared; the Moabites became David's servants and brought tribute.
David also defeated Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah, as he went to restore his power at the river Euphrates.
David captured from him 1,700 horsemen and 20,000 foot soldiers, and he hamstrung all the chariot horses, sparing only enough for a hundred chariots.
When the Arameans of Damascus came to help Hadadezer king of Zobah, David struck down twenty-two thousand of them.
David then put garrisons in Aram of Damascus, and the Arameans became his servants and brought tribute; the LORD gave David victory wherever he went.
David took the shields of gold carried by Hadadezer's officers and brought them to Jerusalem.
From Betah and Berothai, cities belonging to Hadadezer, King David took a great amount of bronze.
When Toi king of Hamath heard that David had defeated the whole army of Hadadezer,
he sent his son Joram to greet David and bless him for fighting against Hadadezer, since Hadadezer had often been at war with Toi; Joram brought with him articles of silver, gold, and bronze.
King David dedicated these also to the LORD, along with the silver and gold he had dedicated from all the nations he had subdued —
from Edom, Moab, the Ammonites, the Philistines, Amalek, and the spoil of Hadadezer son of Rehob, king of Zobah.
David made a name for himself when he returned from striking down eighteen thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt.
He put garrisons throughout all Edom, and all the Edomites became David's servants; the LORD gave David victory wherever he went.
So David reigned over all Israel, administering justice and equity to all his people.
Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army, and Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests, and Seraiah was secretary;
and Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and the Pelethites, and David's sons were priests.
